reasoning: The defining feature of the question's 'Yes' criteria is that any new or expanded export controls "must apply broadly, and must not be targeted or limited to a single country or entity." Evidence from May 2025 indicates that the administration specifically rescinded the broad, worldwide AI model and advanced chip export controls formalized in January 2025 precisely because they were too broad. Instead, BIS is relying on a framework of targeted enforcement (such as 'red flags' and due diligence directed at end-users in countries of concern, like China). Any upcoming export controls will highly likely follow this targeted pattern, which actively disqualifies them from counting as a 'Yes'. Furthermore, with the knowledge cutoff in late October, there is very little time—about two months—to formally propose and implement a complete 180-degree reversal of the May policy. The crowd forecast of 0.30 likely overestimates the probability of broad controls or captures a tail risk around new security legislation, but the explicit policy pivot to targeted end-user restrictions pushes the true probability much lower.
batch_queries: Will the United States implement new or expanded export controls on advanced AI chips, cloud access to AI computing, and/or AI model weights in 2025?
